primary_hypothesis: Adding an independently modelled typhoon-driven ocean-loading term improves held-out superconducting-gravimeter residuals and has a positive, event-consistent attribution coefficient after atmospheric and hydrological controls.

decision_rule.success: The data and closure gates pass, the positive ocean coefficient survives event-block uncertainty, and the fully held-out event improves both RMSE and explained variance at the frozen preprocessing and model settings.
decision_rule.model_failure_branch: A predicted signal with poor gravimeter closure becomes an evaluation of coastal ocean/reanalysis resolution or preprocessing, without a detection claim.
decision_rule.single_event_branch: One successful event is a case study and cannot support cross-typhoon generalization.
decision_rule.null_branch: A physically predicted but undetected signal is retained to constrain station distance, competing hydrology, product resolution, and direct-versus-elastic cancellation.
